DCGI is the head of the Central Drugs Standard Control Organization (CDSCO) and responsible for approval of new drugs and clinical trials.
India is committed to eliminating several NTDs by 2030.
India is a global leader in vaccine manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of developing indigenous vaccines for Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in the context of India's public health challenges.
Analyze the role of public-private partnerships in accelerating vaccine research, development, and ensuring accessibility in resource-limited settings.
Examine the broader implications of this breakthrough for India's pharmaceutical industry and its contribution to global health security.
Evaluate the challenges in eradicating NTDs, including surveillance, access to healthcare, and community engagement.
Suggest policy measures to strengthen India's R&D ecosystem for neglected diseases and ensure equitable distribution of new health innovations.
GS3.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat examining vaccine vials, symbolizing medical research.
GS3.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) identifies 20 NTDs that affect over a billion people worldwide.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New Digital Portal to Streamline Processes for MSMEs, Enhance Ease of Doing Business
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) launched a new digital portal aimed at simplifying regulatory compliance and improving ease of doing business for MSMEs.
• The portal offers a single-window interface for various government services, permits, and licenses, reducing paperwork and processing time.
• It integrates with existing platforms like Udyam Registration and GeM (Government e-Marketplace) to provide comprehensive support.
• The initiative seeks to foster entrepreneurship, boost economic growth, and increase the contribution of MSMEs to India's GDP.
• Training modules and helplines are also available to assist MSMEs in leveraging the new platform.
prelimsPoints: MSMEs are defined based on investment in plant and machinery/equipment and turnover.
They contribute significantly to India's GDP, exports, and employment generation.
Udyam Registration is a government portal for registering MSMEs.
GeM is an online platform for public procurement of goods and services.
Ease of Doing Business Index is published by the World Bank (though discontinued in 2021).
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of MSMEs in India's economic growth, employment generation, and regional development.
Analyze the challenges faced by MSMEs in navigating regulatory frameworks and how digital platforms can enhance ease of doing business.
Examine the potential impact of the new portal on fostering entrepreneurship, reducing compliance costs, and improving access to credit for MSMEs.
Evaluate the effectiveness of e-governance initiatives in improving public service delivery and promoting transparency in the business environment.
Suggest further policy reforms and technological interventions needed to unlock the full potential of India's MSME sector.
GS3.imageDescription: A laptop screen displaying a business portal with various digital icons, symbolizing ease of doing business.
GS3.trivia: MSMEs account for about 30% of India's GDP and 45% of its manufacturing output.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economy
prelimsSyllabus.area: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves Updated National Tourism Policy to Boost Sector Growth and Employment
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a comprehensive update to the National Tourism Policy, aiming to position India as a premier global tourist destination.
• The policy focuses on sustainable and responsible tourism, promoting cultural heritage, adventure tourism, and wellness tourism.
• It includes provisions for enhanced infrastructure development, skill training for tourism professionals, and digital promotion strategies.
• The goal is to significantly increase tourist arrivals and foreign exchange earnings, creating millions of jobs.
• Special emphasis is placed on leveraging technology for personalized tourist experiences and safety.
prelimsPoints: The previous National Tourism Policy was formulated in 2002.
Tourism sector is a major contributor to India's GDP and employment.
Swadesh Darshan Scheme and PRASAD Scheme are key initiatives for tourism development.
India aims to achieve USD 1 trillion from the tourism sector by 2047.
The Ministry of Tourism is the nodal agency for the development and promotion of tourism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key features of the updated National Tourism Policy and its potential to transform India's tourism sector.
Analyze how the policy addresses the challenges faced by the tourism industry, including infrastructure gaps, sustainability concerns, and skill deficits.
Examine the socio-economic benefits of a thriving tourism sector, such as employment generation, foreign exchange earnings, and cultural preservation.
Evaluate the role of digital technologies and sustainable practices in shaping the future of tourism in India.
Suggest innovative strategies to promote India as a diverse and attractive global tourist destination, catering to different segments of travelers.
GS3.imageDescription: A collage of famous Indian tourist landmarks, symbolizing diverse tourism potential.
GS3.trivia: The 'Incredible India' campaign was launched by the Ministry of Tourism in 2002 to promote tourism in India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economy
prelimsSyllabus.area: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment

GS3.headline: Government Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical National Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian government announced new measures to strengthen the cybersecurity framework protecting critical national infrastructure sectors, including power, finance, and telecommunications.
• The updated policy emphasizes proactive threat intelligence, mandatory security audits, and enhanced collaboration between government agencies and private sector operators.
• This comes in response to increasing sophisticated cyberattacks targeting essential services globally.
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) will play a central role in monitoring and incident response.
• Capacity building and skill development in cybersecurity for professionals are also a key focus.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) is defined under the IT Act, 2000.
NCIIPC is the nodal agency for protecting CII in India.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
Cybersecurity policy was last updated in 2013.
India faces threats from state-sponsored cyberattacks and cybercriminals.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of cyberattacks to critical national infrastructure and its implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key features of the strengthened cybersecurity framework and how it aims to protect vital sectors like power, finance, and telecommunications.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity architecture, including technological gaps, human resource shortages, and cross-sectoral coordination.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and public-private partnerships in building resilience against global cyber threats.
Suggest policy recommendations for enhancing India's cyber deterrence capabilities and fostering a culture of cybersecurity awareness.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon superimposed on a circuit board, symbolizing cybersecurity for infrastructure.
GS3.trivia: The Stuxnet computer worm, discovered in 2010, is considered the first known cyberweapon designed to target industrial control systems.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Basics of Cyber Security, Challenges to Internal Security
